session_id: operator-dispatched ('it IS daylight... Do check #1: declared-deny'): pfSense check #1, gold finding
class: Declaration-vs-observation CUSTODY test, NOT a firewall-correctness test. Read declared policy + control egress only; subject probe deliberately UNBOUND (operator-directed).

framing.theorem: A declared policy refusal must remain a refusal under observation, and a successful path probe must not silently erase that declaration. The declared denial and an observed reachability cannot both be quietly promoted into one coherent policy claim.
framing.source_typing: loaded block rule = pfSenseDeclaration; rule counters = pfSenseRuntimeReport (self-report, never an independent path observation); NQ probe = ObservedReachability.
framing.asymmetry: Only a SUBJECT got-through (real handshake) is the unambiguous contradiction. A blocked/refused/timeout subject is admissible as declared_deny_observed_blocked ONLY with a passing CONTROL (proves egress). A block-return RST is not a got-through. Absence of a declared rule = cannot_testify_declared_policy_absent, never 'allowed'.

read_only_discovery.method: read-only SSH (pfctl -sr -vv; ifconfig; pkg/ rc.d checks). NO -f/-d, no rule edit, no service control, no config write.
read_only_discovery.topology: WAN igc0 (CGNAT) + LAN igc1 (192.168.68.1) only; no guest/DMZ VLAN -> sushi-k-lan is the only available source vantage.
read_only_discovery.ids_ips: NONE installed (no Suricata/Snort/pfBlocker daemon) -> the prober is not classified as an attacker; low perturbation hazard.
read_only_discovery.declared_deny_found: USER_RULE: pfB_PRI1_v4 -- block return in log quick on igc1 inet from any to <pfB_PRI1_v4> (ridentifier 1770008176). Table populated: 17007 entries. Counters: ~3.19M evaluations, 8 blocked, 0 states (nothing has ever established to a table member).

verdict_ladder: cannot_testify_probe_target_unbound (declared deny present, subject not probed)
cannot_testify_vantage_unbound (subject blocked but control failed -> egress unproven)

live_read_landed.scope: blocked-only: declared policy read + table population + rule counters + control TCP probe to 1.1.1.1:443. Subject DELIBERATELY UNBOUND.
live_read_landed.result: rule custody=present; control 1.1.1.1:443 = reached (egress proven); subject = (unbound), not_attempted; verdict = cannot_testify_probe_target_unbound; perturbation = control_probe_only / no_subject_path_touched.

operator_decision_recorded: Do NOT send a subject SYN to a pfB_PRI1_v4 (malware-blocklist) member to make the specimen spicy -- a tiny packet, but an intentional outbound touch to a named bad host, the wrong kind of 'live'. The product is declaration custody + refusal discipline, fully delivered by the blocked-only read. The active subject probe (declared_deny_observed_* faces) is PARKED for a scratch/lab firewall with a controlled deny rule + benign target (e.g. 8.8.8.8) + captured teardown -- its own daylight perturbation packet. Also a cleaner future public specimen (no 'why is your epistemology paper SYNing a bad IP' reviewer question).
verification: set -o pipefail; cargo test -p nq-monitor -> EXIT=0 (bare), 0 warnings. Core 11 fixtures + transport parser/sink fixtures (rule line, counter line, table/ridentifier selection, absent vs unknown-surface, hostport split, append-only). Build clean after removing unused ProbeMethod::IcmpEcho (YAGNI; declared-deny is TCP-only for got-through/RST discrimination).
privacy: Receipt carries only the rule DECLARATION (label, table NAME pfB_PRI1_v4, counters, interface igc1, source_spec any) -- NEVER table member IPs. runs/ gitignored. Tracked source/doc fixtures use TEST-NET (198.51.100/24, 203.0.113/24) + public 1.1.1.1. Paranoia scan of tracked source+docs for 192.168.68.*/100.6.75.* = clean.
fences_honored: No push (c65bc98 + this governor commit local-only). Read-only (pfctl -sr -vv); no rule edit/-f/-d/service control/config write. No subject probe to any real denied target. No DB write (active-witness lane, receipt-only). No scheduler, no dashboard authority, no Lane B.
parked_next: ACTIVE declared-deny subject probe on a scratch/lab firewall: controlled deny rule + table + benign stable target (e.g. 8.8.8.8) + control-allowed path + bounded TCP/ICMP probe + captured teardown/rollback. Produces the declared_deny_observed_blocked / _reachable faces without touching malware infrastructure.
